evals.prompt: Show me my recent emails from the last 48 hours, excluding newsletters.
evals.expected_output: A deduplicated, newest-first executive table covering Inbox, Sent, Drafts, and Starred, with real Gmail links and a clear note if results are truncated.
assertions: The recent sub-command is selected
The Gmail query excludes promotions, newsletters, noreply senders, and unsubscribe subjects
No more than 10 results are requested per category and no more than 40 total
The output states the Singapore timezone and does not fabricate message data

evals.prompt: Find emails about the Acme renewal from the last 30 days and show me what needs a decision.
evals.expected_output: A topic digest prioritising subject matches and recent threads, with correspondents, coverage period, decision points, links, and suggested next steps.
assertions: The topic sub-command is selected
The search includes the topic and requested timeframe
Threads are deduplicated and decision points are identified
The result follows the topic digest format

evals.prompt: Format the email records already in this conversation as the standard executive email table.
evals.expected_output: The supplied records are rendered using the standard email table without accessing Gmail.
assertions: The format sub-command is selected
No Gmail connector is called when all required data is already present
The table includes numbering, folder or label, participants, subject, time, summary, status, and link

evals.prompt: Monitor my stakeholder emails, but assume no stakeholder list exists in the workspace instructions.
evals.expected_output: A concise clarification offering to add a stakeholder list or show recent emails instead, without inventing contacts.
assertions: No stakeholder identities or addresses are fabricated
The user is offered the recent-email fallback
No connector fallback is attempted

evals.prompt: Draft a reply-all to the selected thread saying I approve the revised terms. Do not send it.
evals.expected_output: A threaded HTML Gmail draft in Christopher's voice, with recipients shown before creation and confirmation that nothing was sent.
assertions: The full thread is read before drafting
To and Cc recipients exclude Christopher, duplicates, and automated addresses
Recipients are shown before the draft is created
A draft is created but no send action is performed

evals.prompt: Respond to the selected thread. The thread contains a reply from chris@sentient.io newer than the latest inbound message.
evals.expected_output: A hard-block response showing that the thread was already answered, with the existing reply time, summary, and link. No draft is created.
assertions: The already-replied check runs before drafting
The response clearly states that no draft was created
No draft or send action is performed

evals.prompt: Respond to the selected thread. Assume a matching Gmail draft already exists for the same thread.
evals.expected_output: A hard-block response linking to the existing draft and refusing to create a duplicate.
assertions: Existing drafts are checked before creation
The existing draft is summarised and linked
No duplicate draft is created

evals.prompt: Reply to this meeting request with my next available time, but assume no Calendar capability is available.
evals.expected_output: A clear explanation that availability cannot be verified, followed by a stop before drafting.
assertions: The skill does not invent an available time
The missing Calendar capability is reported
No draft or send action is performed

evals.prompt: Reply to a sender who asks to meet at the Sentient office next Tuesday at 3 PM. Assume the time is available.
evals.expected_output: A draft that explains Sentient has no physical office and asks whether the sender prefers Google Meet or an in-person location of their choosing.
assertions: The proposed time is validated before drafting
The draft does not imply that a Sentient office exists
The sender is offered a virtual meeting or asked to suggest an in-person location
The result remains a draft and is never sent
